response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with genuine geospatial interest via GISCollective, but the role demands modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill set applied to low-level routing data pipelines — an area where this resume falls significantly short. His only C++ work dates to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; his current and recent trajectory is firmly TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling. The geospatial experience is GIS data collection rather than routing tile generation, traffic signal integration, or navigation pipeline optimization. The technical distance from the core requirements is too large to bridge at the Mid-Senior level this role targets.
strengths: Genuine geospatial and GIS background (GISCollective, Green Map) demonstrates domain familiarity with geo-data processing concepts
Prior exposure to high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and demonstrated ability to work across a broad technical stack
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; no Rust experience mentioned — the role's primary technical requirement is unmet
No routing, navigation guidance, or routing tile pipeline experience; GIS work is data collection tooling, not routing or traffic optimization
Current career trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, AI agents) is moving further from systems-level C++ development, not toward it
